rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical cyclization of O-tethered aryl iodides (alkenyl ethers) with terephthalonitrile to access chromanes/isochromanes bearing arylated quaternary carbons, using a Mg anode and carbon cloth cathode.
electrochemical_role: Cathode reduces terephthalonitrile to its radical anion, which then mediates single-electron reduction of the aryl C–I bond to generate an aryl radical; Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to Mg(II).

key_intermediates.name: aryl radical from C–I cleavage of O-tethered iodoarene
exocyclic tertiary C-radical (II) after 5- or 6-exo-trig cyclization onto the tethered alkene
radical-radical coupling adduct / cyclohexadienyl anion that rearomatizes by loss of CN-

electron_flow: Cathode delivers one electron to terephthalonitrile to give its radical anion, which transfers an electron to the aryl iodide (the iodide has a more negative reduction potential), cleaving C–I to give an aryl radical; 5- or 6-exo-trig cyclization onto the pendant alkene forms an exocyclic alkyl radical that couples with a second terephthalonitrile radical anion, then rearomatizes by extrusion of cyanide to give the ipso-arylated product. Mg anode is sacrificially oxidized to Mg(2+).

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO adduct 9 of the exocyclic alkyl radical detected by HRMS (<5%); TEMPO adducts 10/11 of terephthalonitrile-derived radical species also detected (<5%); reaction suppressed, supporting both aryl/alkyl C-radical and arene radical-anion intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 2a, 1a, 4a
probe_reagents.observation: E_red(2a) = -1.61 V vs Ag/AgCl, less negative than aryl iodobenzene (E_red ≈ -2.5 V), indicating preferential cathodic reduction of terephthalonitrile; reduction peak of 2a grows with increasing [1a], consistent with mediated SET from 2a•- to 1a.

notes: Net transformation is an ipso-cyanide substitution on terephthalonitrile (loss of CN-) coupled to intramolecular aryl-radical cyclization. Selectivity for 5- vs 6-exo-trig set by tether length (m, n). Mg(II) released from the sacrificial anode likely stabilizes the cyclohexadienyl anion and/or cyanide leaving group. No DFT or KIE reported.
